recognized_artifact: no_confirmed_identification; weak structural resemblance only to a generic enumerate-then-narrow iterative decision loop. No prior-knowledge content was used to populate any other field.

entry_conditions.ambiguity: 'start' is treated as the initial state only because it has no inbound transitions in the table; the packet does not label it as initial.

entry_conditions.value: Transition out of 'start' is gated on the condition 'issue present'.
entry_conditions.ambiguity: Behavior when 'issue present' is false is undefined in the packet (no alternative outbound transition from 'start').

exit_conditions.source_text: | reassess_paths | continuation not justified | end | ; | selected_terminal | closure | end |
exit_conditions.ambiguity: 'end' is treated as terminal only because it has no outbound transitions in the table; the packet does not label it as final.

exit_conditions.value: Exit route 1: from 'reassess_paths' on 'continuation not justified' directly to 'end'.
Exit route 2: from 'reassess_paths' on 'one path selected' to 'selected_terminal', then on 'closure' to 'end'.
exit_conditions.source_text: | reassess_paths | one path selected | selected_terminal | ; | selected_terminal | closure | end |
exit_conditions.ambiguity: Whether 'closure' is an actively performed action or an automatic/trivial transition is not stated.

required_steps.ambiguity: Required only in the sense that it is the sole successor of 'start' and the sole successor of 'repeat_cycle'.
It is the only successor state of 'expand_paths' under all three listed conditions.

required_steps.source_text: | gather_input | new differentiating input available | reassess_paths |
required_steps.ambiguity: Reachable only when 'new differentiating input available' holds; behavior otherwise is undefined.

optional_steps.ambiguity: Optional in the sense of being conditionally entered (only on 'threshold not met'); the packet does not mark any step as optional.
Conditionally entered; bypassed entirely on the 'continuation not justified' exit route.

loops.value: Cycle: expand_paths -> gather_input -> reassess_paths -> repeat_cycle -> expand_paths
loops.source_text: | reassess_paths | threshold not met | repeat_cycle | ; | repeat_cycle | continuation | expand_paths |
loops.ambiguity: No iteration bound, counter, or maximum is specified. Loop exit is governed solely by the non-'threshold not met' conditions at 'reassess_paths'.

branches.value: Three-condition fan-out from 'expand_paths' (path_a listed / path_b listed / path_c listed) all converging on 'gather_input'.
branches.source_text: | expand_paths | path_a listed | gather_input | ; | expand_paths | path_b listed | gather_input | ; | expand_paths | path_c listed | gather_input |
branches.ambiguity: Because all three arcs share the same target, it is undetermined whether these are mutually exclusive alternatives, an enumeration requirement (all three listed), or a representation of an unordered set of listed items. This is a genuine multi-reading point; see representation_layer.ambiguities.

branches.value: Three-way decision at 'reassess_paths': 'threshold not met' -> repeat_cycle; 'one path selected' -> selected_terminal; 'continuation not justified' -> end.
branches.source_text: | reassess_paths | threshold not met | repeat_cycle | ; | reassess_paths | one path selected | selected_terminal | ; | reassess_paths | continuation not justified | end |
branches.ambiguity: Evaluation order/priority among the three conditions is unspecified, as is whether they are mutually exclusive or may co-occur.

termination_conditions.value: 'continuation not justified' evaluated at 'reassess_paths' (termination without selection).
termination_conditions.ambiguity: The packet does not define what makes continuation justified or unjustified.

termination_conditions.value: 'one path selected' at 'reassess_paths' followed by 'closure' at 'selected_terminal' (termination with selection).
termination_conditions.source_text: | reassess_paths | one path selected | selected_terminal | ; | selected_terminal | closure | end |
termination_conditions.ambiguity: 'closure' is unconditional in effect (only outbound arc) but its content is undefined.

termination_conditions.value: Possible implicit non-terminating/undefined states: 'gather_input' when new differentiating input is NOT available, and 'start' when 'issue present' is false.
termination_conditions.source_text: | gather_input | new differentiating input available | reassess_paths | (no complementary row); | start | issue present | expand_paths | (no complementary row)
termination_conditions.ambiguity: The packet is silent on these cases; they may be stalls, implicit terminations, or simply out of scope. Not resolved here.

structural_layer.control_flow_shape: Single-entry directed cyclic graph. Linear entry (start -> expand_paths), a convergent fan-out (expand_paths -> gather_input via three conditions), a linear step (gather_input -> reassess_paths), and a three-way decision hub (reassess_paths) that either (a) re-enters the cycle via a pass-through state (repeat_cycle -> expand_paths), (b) routes to a selection state (selected_terminal) that then closes to 'end', or (c) routes directly to 'end'. One cycle; one terminal state ('end') reachable by two distinct routes.

primitive_layer.dominant_primitive: evaluate_against_threshold (the 'reassess_paths' node is the sole multiway decision hub and governs every loop-continuation and every termination route)
candidate_missing_primitives: handling for 'issue present' being false at 'start' (no complementary arc)
handling for 'new differentiating input available' being false at 'gather_input' (no complementary arc; possible stall)
any bound, counter, or exhaustion criterion on the expand/gather/reassess cycle
any definition or measurement primitive for the 'threshold' referenced at 'reassess_paths'
any priority/ordering rule among the three outbound conditions of 'reassess_paths'
any verification, validation, or rollback primitive following 'one path selected'
any state-carrying primitive explaining what persists across iterations (e.g., whether previously listed paths are retained, revised, or re-derived on re-entry to 'expand_paths')
any explicit semantics for 'repeat_cycle' beyond pass-through, and for 'closure' beyond pass-through

invariants.value: Every path from 'start' that terminates does so at 'end'; 'end' is the unique sink in the table.
invariants.ambiguity: Inferred from absence of outbound rows rather than from an explicit terminal marker.

invariants.value: 'reassess_paths' is reachable only via 'gather_input'; 'gather_input' is reachable only via 'expand_paths'; 'expand_paths' is reachable only via 'start' or 'repeat_cycle'.
invariants.ambiguity: Holds only under a closed-world reading of the table (no unlisted transitions exist).

invariants.value: Any iteration must pass through 'gather_input' before returning to 'reassess_paths' (i.e., no re-evaluation without an intervening information-gathering step).
invariants.source_text: | repeat_cycle | continuation | expand_paths | ; | expand_paths | ... | gather_input | ; | gather_input | new differentiating input available | reassess_paths |
invariants.ambiguity: Derived from graph topology, not stated as a rule.

preconditions.value: At least one of {path_a listed, path_b listed, path_c listed} must hold to leave 'expand_paths'.
preconditions.ambiguity: Under an alternative reading, ALL three must hold (conjunctive enumeration requirement) rather than at least one. The packet does not disambiguate.

preconditions.value: 'new differentiating input available' must hold to leave 'gather_input'.
preconditions.source_text: | gather_input | new differentiating input available | reassess_paths |
preconditions.ambiguity: Implies information gathering must yield something differentiating before reassessment; the failure case is unspecified.

preconditions.value: 'continuation' must hold to leave 'repeat_cycle' back into 'expand_paths'.
preconditions.ambiguity: Relationship between 'continuation' here and 'continuation not justified' at 'reassess_paths' is unstated; they may or may not be complementary evaluations of the same predicate.

postconditions.value: On the selection route, exactly one path has been selected before 'selected_terminal' is entered.
postconditions.ambiguity: 'one' is taken literally from the condition text; multi-selection is not represented.

postconditions.value: On the non-selection route, the procedure ends without any path having been selected.
postconditions.ambiguity: The packet does not state what, if anything, is produced on this route.

stopping_criteria.value: 'one path selected' at 'reassess_paths' (leads to terminal selection state).

stopping_criteria.ambiguity: Criteria for justification are not defined.

stopping_criteria.value: Negation: 'threshold not met' is the sole non-stopping outcome, and it forces continued iteration.
stopping_criteria.ambiguity: Whether 'threshold met' is equivalent to 'one path selected' is not stated; they may be the same predicate expressed differently or two independent predicates.

validity_conditions.value: The table is closed-world: only the ten listed transitions are permitted.
validity_conditions.source_text: packet presents a complete transition table with no default/else rows
validity_conditions.ambiguity: The packet does not state whether it is exhaustive; an open-world reading would leave unlisted condition cases undefined rather than forbidden.

validity_conditions.value: Reassessment is valid only when it follows the acquisition of new differentiating input.
validity_conditions.source_text: | gather_input | new differentiating input available | reassess_paths |
validity_conditions.ambiguity: Stated as a transition guard, not as an explicit validity rule.

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: Given that an issue is present, the procedure moves from start to a state in which paths are expanded; three conditions (path_a listed, path_b listed, path_c listed) each lead from expansion to an input-gathering state. Once new differentiating input is available, the procedure reassesses the paths. Reassessment has three outcomes: if a threshold is not met, the procedure enters a repeat_cycle state and, on continuation, returns to path expansion; if one path is selected, the procedure moves to a selected_terminal state and then closes to end; if continuation is not justified, the procedure ends directly. The single cycle (expand_paths -> gather_input -> reassess_paths -> repeat_cycle -> expand_paths) has no stated bound.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: Trigger-gated entry into an unbounded expand/gather/reassess loop, whose single multiway decision node either re-enters the loop, exits via a selection-commitment state, or exits directly without selection; both exits converge on one terminal state.

ambiguities.description: Three distinct conditions all map to the same next_state, so the table alone does not determine the logical relation among them.
plausible_interpretations: Disjunctive/mutually exclusive branch: any one of path_a/path_b/path_c being listed suffices to proceed.
Conjunctive enumeration requirement: expansion is complete only when all three paths are listed, and the three rows redundantly encode a single 'all listed' guard.

Set-membership/iteration encoding: 'path_x listed' represents an unordered set of enumerated items with cardinality three in this instance, not a fixed three-way branch (i.e., the count 3 may be incidental rather than structural).
Degenerate branch: the three conditions are structurally irrelevant to control flow since the target is identical, and the branching is representational rather than procedural.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Recorded as a convergent fan-out with unresolved logical relation. Structural layer lists it under branches; constraint layer states the precondition under both the 'at least one' and 'all three' readings without choosing between them. No single reading was adopted to simplify the AST.

ambiguities.description: Three conditions with different targets, but no stated evaluation order and no stated mutual exclusivity.
plausible_interpretations: Mutually exclusive predicates evaluated in any order.
Priority-ordered guards evaluated top-to-bottom as listed in the table.
Potentially co-occurring predicates with unspecified tie-breaking (e.g., 'threshold not met' and 'continuation not justified' could both hold).
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: AST marks evaluation_order and mutual_exclusivity as 'unspecified'. No arbitration rule was invented.

ambiguities.description: The two conditions use overlapping vocabulary but sit at different states.
plausible_interpretations: They are complementary evaluations of one predicate, with repeat_cycle re-checking it before looping back.
They are independent predicates that happen to share a lexeme.
'continuation' at repeat_cycle is vacuous/unconditional, since it is the only outbound arc.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Both conditions recorded verbatim; no identity between them asserted.

ambiguities.description: Each state has a single guarded outbound arc with no complementary arc for the negative case.
plausible_interpretations: The negative case is an implicit stall/wait state (remain until the condition holds).
The negative case is an implicit termination not shown in the table.
The negative case is simply out of scope for this packet.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Recorded as undefined_in_packet in the AST and as a possible implicit non-terminating state in termination_conditions. No default edge was added.

ambiguities.description: These are pass-through nodes/conditions with exactly one outbound arc each; whether they denote actions or bookkeeping is not stated.
plausible_interpretations: They denote substantive actions (e.g., a commitment step, a closing step) that the table abstracts away.
They are structural bookkeeping nodes with no procedural content, present only to mark loop back-edges and terminal routes.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Retained as states in both the structural layer and the AST; no action semantics attributed to them.

recognition_analysis.recognition_basis: The control-flow shape (trigger -> enumerate multiple candidate paths -> gather differentiating information -> reassess against a threshold -> either iterate, commit to one candidate, or stop) is a general enumerate-and-narrow pattern that resembles families of iterative narrowing procedures. Recognition was structural only, and no specific artifact identification is asserted. No recognized-artifact content was used to populate the structural, primitive, constraint, or representation layers.
recognition_analysis.prior_knowledge_leakage_risk: low — all state names, condition names, and edges were taken verbatim from the packet; abstraction labels in the primitive layer (e.g., 'enumerate_options', 'evaluate_against_threshold') are derived directly from packet condition text ('path_x listed', 'threshold not met') and introduce no domain vocabulary. Residual risk is that the primitive labels themselves impose a slightly more purposive reading than the bare table warrants.

confidence.structural_layer: high — the graph structure is fully determined by the ten rows; the only uncertainty is the logical relation among same-target arcs and the closed-world assumption.
confidence.primitive_layer: medium — transitions are transcribed with high confidence, but primitive labels and 'dominant_primitive' involve interpretive abstraction over undefined state semantics.
confidence.constraint_layer: medium — preconditions and stopping criteria follow directly from guards, but invariants and validity conditions rest on a closed-world reading the packet does not confirm.
confidence.representation_layer: medium-high — the AST mirrors the table faithfully and marks unresolved points as unspecified; the loop-versus-fanout framing is one of several defensible framings.
confidence.recognition_analysis: high — recognition status and its (non-)use in extraction are reported directly.

notes: Extraction is verbatim-anchored: every state, condition, and edge is taken from the ten table rows. Deliberate non-normalizations: (1) the three 'expand_paths' arcs were NOT collapsed into a single 'paths listed' guard despite that being the cleaner representation, because the packet does not establish whether the relation is disjunctive, conjunctive, or incidental; (2) 'repeat_cycle' and 'selected_terminal' were NOT elided as pass-through nodes despite adding no branching, because eliding them would discard packet-supported structure; (3) no default/else edges were added for the unguarded negative cases at 'start', 'gather_input', 'repeat_cycle', and 'selected_terminal'. The packet supplies no iteration bound, no threshold definition, no ordering among the three reassessment conditions, and no domain terminology; none of these were supplied from outside the packet. The count of three enumerated paths may be structural or incidental — this is not resolvable from the packet and is recorded as an open ambiguity.
